steps.label: Trigger exposure
steps.description: Environmental trigger or allergen enters the airway.

steps.label: IgE recognition
steps.description: Allergen interacts with allergen-specific IgE already bound to mast cells and basophils.

steps.label: Cross-linking and degranulation
steps.description: Receptor-bound IgE cross-links, causing immediate release of histamine and other preformed mediators.

steps.label: Lipid mediator synthesis
steps.description: Leukotrienes and prostaglandins are synthesized and released.

steps.label: Functional effect
steps.description: Smooth muscle contracts, mucus secretion increases, vascular leakage occurs, and acute airflow limitation develops.

steps.label: Th2 cytokine signaling
steps.description: Th2 cells generate IL-4, IL-5, IL-13 and related cytokines after allergic activation.

steps.label: Cell recruitment
steps.description: Eosinophils, basophils, neutrophils, and T cells localize to the airway over the next several hours.

steps.label: Mediator release
steps.description: Inflammatory cells release cytokines, leukotrienes, cationic proteins, and growth factors.

steps.label: Persistent inflammation
steps.description: Bronchoconstriction, mucus production, epithelial injury, and edema continue beyond the immediate attack.

steps.label: Remodeling risk
steps.description: Repeated inflammation promotes basement membrane thickening, fibrosis, hyperplasia, and more fixed obstruction over time.

steps.label: Receptor binding
steps.description: Beta2 agonist binds beta2 adrenergic receptor on airway smooth muscle.

steps.label: Gs activation
steps.description: Receptor activation couples to Gs protein and stimulates adenylate cyclase.

steps.label: Second messenger increase
steps.description: ATP is converted to cAMP, increasing intracellular cAMP concentration.

steps.label: Protein kinase activation
steps.description: cAMP activates protein kinase A and reduces intracellular calcium availability.

steps.label: Smooth muscle relaxation
steps.description: Myosin light-chain kinase activity falls, myosin light-chain phosphatase activity rises, and bronchodilation occurs.

steps.label: Receptor blockade
steps.description: Drug blocks parasympathetic muscarinic receptors in the airway, especially the M3 effect relevant to asthma.

steps.label: Cholinergic signal reduction
steps.description: Bronchoconstrictor acetylcholine signaling is diminished.

steps.label: Calcium signaling decreases
steps.description: Intracellular signaling leads to reduced calcium-related contraction pathways.

steps.label: Secretory effect
steps.description: Mucus secretion and mucosal edema fall along with cholinergic tone.

steps.label: Net effect
steps.description: Airway smooth muscle relaxes and bronchodilation improves, especially when combined with a beta2 agonist.

steps.label: Cell entry
steps.description: Inhaled corticosteroid enters airway inflammatory and structural cells.

steps.label: Glucocorticoid receptor activation
steps.description: Drug binds glucocorticoid receptor and influences nuclear gene transcription.

steps.label: Anti-inflammatory gene expression
steps.description: Annexin-1, secretory leukoprotease inhibitor, and IL-10 expression increase.

steps.label: Inflammatory cell suppression
steps.description: Recruitment, adhesion, survival, and mediator release of eosinophils, mast cells, dendritic cells, and T lymphocytes decrease.

steps.label: Clinical effect
steps.description: Airway hyperresponsiveness and exacerbation frequency decrease, improving long-term control.

steps.label: Phosphodiesterase inhibition
steps.description: Theophylline decreases degradation of cyclic nucleotides.

steps.label: cAMP accumulation
steps.description: Intracellular cAMP rises abnormally in overdose.

steps.label: Systemic overstimulation
steps.description: CNS excitation and cardiovascular stimulation increase.

steps.label: Clinical toxicity
steps.description: Seizures, tachycardia, and hemodynamic instability develop.

steps.label: Countermeasure
steps.description: A cardioselective beta-blocker can reduce cAMP formation via adenylate cyclase suppression while limiting asthma risk compared with nonselective blockade.
